assumptions_to_verify: Runtime-native receipts are trustworthy and correspond to the reviewed worktree snapshot.
Production deployments run the Alembic migration chain in the same order as the inline schema.
lesson_task_class is populated on normal workflow routes so trend grouping does not collapse to general.
contradictions_checked: Claude said tests were not run in its review env, but supervisor runtime receipts for outcome_review report passed tests; I treat Claude test_status unknown as reviewer-local, not gate-level.
Prompt tdd.md receipt hash mismatched current file; current handoff, outcome-review, skill-receipts, and local shasum agree, indicating stale packet evidence.
Observed run-end wiring records quality_trends in a try/except and returns gate_authority unchanged, with no call path that advances or blocks gates from metrics.
Status precedence in _payload_accepted checks supervisor_final_status before status and claude_gate_status, and the regression test covers Claude accepted versus supervisor blocked.
Query methods are SELECT-only and tests assert event/row count invariance for query and CLI query.
missing_evidence: Live Postgres migration run with CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN set.
Live Postgres upsert/query/audit helper exercise for supervisor_quality_trends, not just schema shape.
Fresh local pytest rerun by this reviewer; current decision relies on runtime-native isolated-worktree receipts.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that Postgres production behavior is only structurally supported here: table DDL, Alembic migration text, and inline-schema equivalence are present, but live Postgres migration/helper execution was not demonstrated in the inspected evidence.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing focused pytest run, a live Postgres migration/helper failure, evidence that quality trend recording can alter gate/workflow authority, or a current artifact hash mismatch in the handoff/outcome-review records would change this to revise.

claims: P1 persisted per-gate trend rows derived from ledger - verified by source and 3 metric tests
P2 sampled P11 audit catches missing deliverable false accept - verified by git fixture test
P3 read-only query and CLI with count invariance - verified by API and CLI tests
P4 metrics observational-only - verified by gate/workflow/step invariance test and stdio try/except wiring
Postgres/SQLite structural alignment - verified by inline-vs-migration equivalence test
Claude postgres parity test name supervisor_quality_trends_parity is incorrect
confidence_criteria: file ownership exact vs deliverables: pass
live pytest execution in Cursor review: pass (11/11)
live Postgres alembic upgrade: not performed (DSN-gated skip)
outcome.confidence_rationale: Live pytest verified 11/11 focused tests; source wiring and P1-P4 traceability confirmed by direct read. Postgres live migration and misnamed parity test reference remain minor gaps.

outcome.confidence_rationale: Source symbols, wiring, file ownership, and 8+2 non-vacuous tests verified by direct read; assertions are concrete and resistant to fake-pass. Not confirmed by a live pytest run (approval denied) and Postgres parity is structural only.

migration applies cleanly on a live Postgres deployment

specialists.objection: pytest not run in review env -> test_status self_reported

outcome.summary: Quality-trend subsystem persists per-gate ledger-derived metrics, exposes read-only task_class/gate query + CLI, and runs sampled P11 false-accept audit. All 4 PRD promises (P1-P4) map to non-vacuous tests; file ownership exact (10 files, +566/-40 tracked + 4 untracked impl); observational-only confirmed in source and stdio run-end wiring. pytest not executable in review env -> self_reported.
